name: Rio de Janeiro
localTips: Get a Riocard (or tap a contactless card) for the Metrô — it's the fastest, safest way between Copacabana, Ipanema and downtown, especially at night when traffic and buses get unpredictable.
Leave the flashy jewelry and don't flash your phone on the beach or in Lapa at night — locals carry cash in a pocket, not a bag, and only bring what you need for the day.
December–March is hot, crowded and prone to sudden tropical downpours; June–August is 'winter' — still mild and sunny most days, with far thinner crowds and cheaper hotels.
Tipping isn't obligatory — restaurants add a 10% service charge to the bill automatically ('serviço incluso'), and rounding up for a boteco tab or a taxi is appreciated but not expected.
A caipirinha at a boteco is the classic order, but ask for it 'não muito forte' if you want less cachaça — bars pour generously and the sugar hides the strength.

activities.title: Copacabana beach & Avenida Atlântica
activities.description: The wave-patterned Portuguese-stone promenade runs 4km along Rio's most famous beach — kiosks, footvolley games and the curve of the bay from Leme to Arpoador.

activities.title: Sunset at Arpoador rock
activities.description: The rocky outcrop between Ipanema and Copacabana where locals gather every clear evening to applaud the sunset — genuinely, the whole beach claps when the sun drops behind the hills.

activities.title: Selarón Steps
activities.description: Chilean artist Jorge Selarón tiled 215 steps between Lapa and Santa Teresa in a mosaic of over 2,000 tiles from 60 countries — his life's work until his death in 2013.

activities.title: Santa Teresa hillside streets
activities.description: Cobblestone lanes, colonial mansions and artists' studios on a tram-served hill above downtown — Rio's bohemian quarter, with skyline glimpses between the bougainvillea.

activities.title: Arcos da Lapa & Friday street party
activities.description: A white Portuguese aqueduct turned tram bridge anchors Lapa's nightlife district — Friday nights the street below fills for free with samba, forró and beer sold from coolers.

activities.title: Parque Lage
activities.description: A romantic 1920s mansion housing the fine arts school sits inside a jungle garden at the foot of Corcovado — free to wander, with Christ the Redeemer peeking through the treetops.

activities.title: Prainha surfer's beach
activities.description: A small, wild horseshoe beach past São Conrado backed by Atlantic rainforest — Rio's best surf break, protected from development, with a fraction of the crowds of Copacabana.

activities.title: Praça Mauá & the revitalized port
activities.description: The old port's warehouses have become a waterfront plaza framed by the swooping white curves of the Museu do Amanhã — free to walk the promenade and photograph the building's spines.

activities.title: Confeitaria Colombo
activities.description: An 1894 Belle Époque café of jacaranda mirrors and Belgian stained glass in downtown Rio — free to walk in and admire, though the pastries make it hard to leave empty-handed.

activities.title: Tijuca National Forest hike
activities.description: The world's largest urban forest, hand-replanted in the 1860s after being clear-cut for coffee — waterfalls, howler monkeys and free trailheads leading up toward Pico da Tijuca.

activities.title: Mirante Dona Marta viewpoint
activities.description: A free lookout with a full-body Christ the Redeemer silhouette and the whole bay laid out below — the postcard shot everyone pays for at Corcovado, framed for nothing up here.

activities.title: Forte de Copacabana
activities.description: A working army fort at the far end of Copacabana beach with the best south-facing view of the coastline — the plaza and cannons are free, the small military museum inside is a few reais.

activities.title: Leme boardwalk & Morro do Leme
activities.description: The quieter northern tip of Copacabana beach, backed by a forested hill with a short free trail to Forte Duque de Caxias — a locals' stretch of sand away from the main crowds.

activities.title: Monday-night samba at Pedra do Sal
activities.description: A stone outcrop where enslaved Africans once unloaded salt is now the birthplace of Rio samba — a free, packed street party of live roda de samba every Monday and Friday evening.
activities.insiderNote: Go on a Monday for the more traditional roda de samba crowd; arrive by 7pm to find room on the steps before it's standing-room only. Bring small bills for the beer coolers.

activities.title: Feira de São Cristóvão
activities.description: A cavernous covered pavilion recreating a Northeastern Brazilian street fair — forró bands, cowhide crafts and food from Bahia to Pernambuco, open all weekend till dawn.
activities.insiderNote: It runs Tuesday to Sunday but really comes alive Friday and Saturday nights when live forró bands play till sunrise — small entry fee, cash for stalls.

activities.title: Sunset happy hour at Bar Urca
activities.description: Locals buy a beer and a pastel from the counter, then sit on the seawall across the street with Sugarloaf and Guanabara Bay right in front of them — Rio's most scenic cheap happy hour.
activities.insiderNote: Skip the sit-down tables — buy from the counter and grab a spot on the low wall by the water like everyone else does. Arrive by 5pm on a Friday to get a seat before it's packed.

activities.title: Chopp and petiscos at a classic boteco
activities.description: Amarelinho on Cinelândia square is the archetypal Rio boteco — ice-cold draft chopp, bolinho de bacalhau and outdoor tables facing the Theatro Municipal, packed with office workers after 6pm.
activities.insiderNote: Order chopp, not garrafa beer — draft is fresher and what locals actually drink here. Petiscos (small plates) are meant to be shared around the table, not ordered solo.

activities.title: Sunset hike to Mirante do Vidigal
activities.description: A short climb through the Vidigal favela leads to a bar-lined lookout with arguably Rio's best sunset view — Ipanema, Leblon and Dois Irmãos spread out below, mostly a local secret.
activities.insiderNote: Go with a guide or a moto-taxi up from the favela entrance rather than walking alone after dark; Bar David and Alto Vidigal are the two lookout bars locals actually use.

activities.title: Saturday feijoada at Bar do Mineiro
activities.description: A black-and-white-tiled Santa Teresa institution serving the definitive Rio feijoada — the black bean and pork stew that's a Saturday ritual across the whole city.
activities.insiderNote: Feijoada is traditionally a Saturday-only, midday-to-afternoon meal — go hungry, order it with a caipirinha, and expect a long, unhurried lunch, not a quick bite.

activities.title: Live samba at Trapiche Gamboa
activities.description: A converted 19th-century warehouse in the old port district hosts some of Rio's most respected samba and choro musicians — a real, dressed-up night out away from the tourist circuit.
activities.insiderNote: Book a table in advance for weekend shows — the room is small and fills with regulars who've been coming for years. Wednesdays often have a cheaper, looser session.

activities.title: Céu na Terra hillside bar
activities.description: A tiny open-air bar wedged into a Santa Teresa hillside with plastic chairs, cheap caipirinhas and one of the best sunset views in the neighborhood — no sign, just follow the crowd.
activities.insiderNote: There's no seating guarantee — locals stand on the steps below with a drink. It only really gets going after 6pm; cash only.

activities.title: Ferry day trip to Ilha de Paquetá
activities.description: A car-free island an hour by ferry across Guanabara Bay — horse-drawn carriages, crumbling 19th-century mansions and a slow, sleepy pace most visitors never discover.
activities.insiderNote: Take the slower conventional ferry, not the express catamaran, for a cheaper ticket and better bay views; rent a bike on the island instead of a carriage to see more of it.

activities.title: Late-night pastel at a Lapa pastelaria
activities.description: Fried, thin-crusted pastéis stuffed with cheese or ground beef, sold from streetside counters around Lapa until the early hours — the classic way to end a night of samba.
activities.insiderNote: Order pastel de queijo with a caldo de cana (fresh sugarcane juice) pressed right at the counter — the pairing is a Rio institution and costs almost nothing.

activities.title: Christ the Redeemer via Corcovado train
activities.description: A cog train climbs through Tijuca Forest to the base of the 38-meter Art Deco statue, one of the New Seven Wonders — arms outstretched over the whole city and bay.
activities.bookingQuery: Christ the Redeemer Corcovado train ticket Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Sugarloaf Mountain cable car
activities.description: Two glass cable cars lift you in stages to the granite dome of Pão de Açúcar — sunset is the classic slot, watching the city light up as Corcovado glows across the bay.
activities.bookingQuery: Sugarloaf Mountain cable car ticket Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Jardim Botânico
activities.description: A 140-hectare botanical garden of imperial palm avenues, giant Amazon water lilies and a fragrant garden for the visually impaired — founded by the Portuguese royal family in 1808.
activities.bookingQuery: Jardim Botanico Rio de Janeiro entry ticket

activities.title: Museu do Amanhã
activities.description: Santiago Calatrava's wing-shaped 'Museum of Tomorrow' on the port explores climate change and humanity's future through immersive, data-driven exhibits — Rio's most striking modern building.
activities.bookingQuery: Museu do Amanha ticket Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Guided tour of Rocinha favela
activities.description: A local guide leads you through Latin America's largest favela — home to over 100,000 people, with its own economy, murals and a viewpoint over the whole South Zone.
activities.bookingQuery: Rocinha favela guided tour Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Football match at Maracanã
activities.description: The stadium that hosted two World Cup finals — Flamengo or Fluminense on matchday means flares, drums and a full-throated crowd unlike any stadium atmosphere in the world.
activities.bookingQuery: football match tickets Maracana stadium Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Tandem hang gliding over São Conrado
activities.description: Launch off the Pedra Bonita ramp with an instructor strapped to your back and soar over the rainforest, landing on São Conrado beach — Rio's signature adrenaline bucket-list flight.
activities.bookingQuery: tandem hang gliding Sao Conrado Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Samba show at Carioca da Gema
activities.description: One of Lapa's original live samba houses — a small stage, a serious house band, and a floor that fills for dancing by the second set, in a converted 19th-century townhouse.
activities.bookingQuery: samba show Carioca da Gema Lapa Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Helicopter tour over Rio
activities.description: Six to eight minutes over Christ the Redeemer, Sugarloaf and the full curve of the coastline — the fastest way to grasp just how the mountains, forest and sea interlock here.
activities.neighborhood: Departs Lagoa or Morro da Urca

activities.title: Museu de Arte do Rio (MAR)
activities.description: A colonial palace fused to a swooping modern wing on Praça Mauá — Brazilian art from colonial times to street art, with a rooftop garden looking straight across the bay.
activities.bookingQuery: Museu de Arte do Rio MAR ticket

activities.title: Guanabara Bay sunset schooner cruise
activities.description: A slow sail past Sugarloaf, the naval islands and under the Rio–Niterói bridge as the sky turns orange behind the skyline — drinks included, the water view of Rio you don't get on land.
activities.bookingQuery: Guanabara Bay sunset schooner cruise Rio de Janeiro

activities.title: Guided sunrise hike to Pedra da Gávea
activities.description: A tough, rope-assisted scramble to the summit of the world's largest coastal monolith — a 360° panorama over the entire city, best with a guide who knows the exposed final pitch.
activities.bookingQuery: guided hike Pedra da Gavea Rio de Janeiro
